query: SELECT ps.id, ps.protocolId, ps.sortOrder, ps.title, ps.frequency, ps.timeOfDay, ps.description FROM protocol_steps ps LIMIT 5;
command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 4Loc9eiqTeCpHbm.bfc0203d8e87 --database 2VRczM8SEoMgkRv9pxV2Z3 --execute SELECT ps.id, ps.protocolId, ps.sortOrder, ps.title, ps.frequency, ps.timeOfDay, ps.description FROM protocol_steps ps LIMIT 5;

rows.title: Daily Carb Target — 110 g Maximum
rows.description: Track total carbohydrate intake using a food diary or app. Maximum 110 g net carbs per day. Carb sources should come exclusively from single-ingredient whole foods: sweet potatoes, white rice, berries, seasonal fruit. No bread, pasta, cereal, or packaged carbs.

rows.title: Protein Target — 1 g per lb Lean Mass
rows.description: Prioritize animal protein from single-ingredient sources: beef, chicken, fish, eggs, organ meats, bone broth. Target minimum 1 g protein per pound of lean body mass. Each meal should center around a protein source.

rows.title: Single-Ingredient Rule — Every Meal
rows.description: Every food item on your plate should be a single ingredient: steak, eggs, salmon, sweet potato, avocado, butter, olive oil, berries. If it has a nutrition label with multiple ingredients, do not eat it. Cook with butter, ghee, tallow, or olive oil only.

rows.title: Electrolyte Supplementation
rows.description: Low-carb diets increase electrolyte excretion. Supplement daily: sodium (2–3 g from salt or broth), potassium (from avocado, meat), magnesium (400 mg glycinate). Prevents headaches, cramps, and fatigue.

rows.title: Daily Food Journal
rows.description: Log all meals with estimated macros. Note energy levels, hunger/satiety, digestion, and any cravings. This data helps the provider fine-tune the protocol.
